summary_rows.selection_rationale: Reached coded convergence and is the strongest documented Salt 1 restart candidate, but still not steady enough.
summary_rows.determination: case may look flat, but residual heat-balance floor or late-window drift remains too large for a clean steady-state claim

summary_rows.selection_rationale: Current manuscript representative and active continuation target.
summary_rows.determination: late-window drift is tiny and net heat-balance residual is below 0.5% of heater power; active continuation is still running, so this is usable now but still worth refreshing later

hypothesis_rows.hypothesis: Salt 1 primarily needs a longer continuation with the repaired MPI bootstrap.
hypothesis_rows.why_it_matters: Both Salt 1 rows are flat in time, but neither has been extended under the repaired post-June-2 continuation path.
hypothesis_rows.test_to_run: Continue Salt 1 Jin from 3229 s and Salt 1 Kirst from 3279.163522013 s with the corrected OpenFOAM bootstrap.
hypothesis_rows.expected_signal: If the 4% net-Q floor is mostly a runtime issue, the residual should fall materially without a large setup change.

hypothesis_rows.hypothesis: Salt 1 is a low-power modeling problem, not only a runtime problem.
hypothesis_rows.why_it_matters: Salt 1 remains much worse than Salt 2-4 on temperature, mdot, and ambient-loss metrics simultaneously.
hypothesis_rows.test_to_run: Only after the continuation retries finish, test a targeted Salt 1 loss-model or cooler-h sensitivity rather than a blanket property sweep.
hypothesis_rows.expected_signal: If the residual floor survives the new continuation, the setup itself is the next lever, not more runtime alone.
